headline: Coleman's Year 3, Leonhard's Defense Set Bills Apart
writeup: Buffalo Bills training camp is officially underway with excitement building on multiple fronts, as open practice tickets at Highmark Stadium go on sale and fans prepare their best tailgating spreads for the Joe Brady era. The wide receiver room is under the microscope with DJ Moore's arrival, Trent Sherfield's scouting report drawing attention, and a newly claimed wideout who scored the final points at old Highmark Stadium adding intrigue to a loaded depth chart. On the offensive line, Dion Dawkins — who controversially theorized the stadium is cursed and tied to Buffalo's Super Bowl struggles — is back anchoring a unit with a fresh Top 100 honoree and a camp competition brewing for a new starter, while Brandon Beane's linebacker market maneuver and the signing of a veteran cornerback headline a quietly impressive offseason. Questions loom large over which starter could lose his job, whether young players ranked near the league's bottom can rise, and whether Josh Allen — facing nightmare injury speculation and chasing immortality — can finally carry this franchise past a playoff ceiling that risks tying an unwanted NFL record. With five defining games on the 2026 schedule, oddsmakers already weighing in on the AFC East race, and a third-round draft pick and rookie spotlight on Joe Brady demanding attention, the stakes for Buffalo have never felt higher.
